customAttributes.name: Wrap Actions
customAttributes.helpText: This will enable action buttons to wrap when a column or container is too thin for the contents.

customAttributes.name: Spacing
customAttributes.helpText: Large (8px) spacing between actions.

customAttributes.name: Size
customAttributes.helpText: Sets the size of the specific actions.

customAttributes.name: CSS Classes
customAttributes.helpText: Enter classes to add to this component. You may add multiple classes by separating them with a space.

customAttributes.name: Description
customAttributes.helpText: Enter a short description of the image/icon or select a source column from the quick pick options. This will be used as title & alt text for the avatar image/icon.

customAttributes.name: Icon
customAttributes.helpText: Enter an icon class or use the Pick Icon dialog to select the icon you want to add to display in the avatar.

customAttributes.name: Image
customAttributes.helpText:
Use the Media popup dialog to select a source for the avatar image.

Available options include:

  • URL
  • BLOB Column
  • URL Column

customAttributes.name: Initials
customAttributes.helpText: Select the source column to be displayed within each avatar. Only varchar2 data types are supported.

customAttributes.name: Shape
customAttributes.helpText: Select the shape of the avatar. Default inherits the shape set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Size
customAttributes.helpText: Set the size of the avatar. The default inherits the sizing set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Spacing
customAttributes.helpText: This option will create spacing between the avatars or allow them to stack on top of each other.

customAttributes.name: Type
customAttributes.helpText: Select the type of content that will be displayed in the avatar.

customAttributes.name: Icon
customAttributes.helpText: Enter an icon class or use the Pick Icon dialog to select the icon you want to add to display in the badge.

customAttributes.name: Label
customAttributes.helpText: Enter a value for the badge label or select a source column from the quick pick options.

customAttributes.name: Display Label
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to display or hide the badge label. When set to Yes, the badge label will be displayed in addition to the badge value. When set to No, only the badge value will be displayed.

customAttributes.name: Shape
customAttributes.helpText: Select the shape of the badge. Default inherits the shape set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Size
customAttributes.helpText: Set the size of the badge. The default inherits the sizing set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: State
customAttributes.helpText: Select the source column for the state of the badge. Substitute column value with "danger", "warning", "success" or "info" for default styling.

customAttributes.name: Style
customAttributes.helpText: Select the badge style. Default inherits the style set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Value
customAttributes.helpText: Select a source column for the badge value.

customAttributes.name: Alignment
customAttributes.helpText: Align the comment by passing a value of "inbound" (appearing on the side where comments typically are received) or "outbound" (positioned on the opposite side, where comments are typically sent).

customAttributes.name: Apply Theme Colors
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to use the Universal Theme colors for avatar and badge row items. When set to Yes, the u-colors class will be applied to each row. When set to No, a default color will be used instead.

customAttributes.name: Attributes
customAttributes.helpText: Enter some text and/or column substitution strings to be used as the comment attributes.

customAttributes.name: CSS Classes
customAttributes.helpText: Enter CSS classes to add to the visual. You may add multiple classes by separating them with a space.

customAttributes.name: Description
customAttributes.helpText: Enter a short description of the image/icon or select a source column from the quick pick options. This will be used as title & alt text for the avatar image/icon.

customAttributes.name: Icon
customAttributes.helpText: Enter an icon class or use the Pick Icon dialog to select the icon you want to add to display in the avatar.

customAttributes.name: Image
customAttributes.helpText:
Use the Media popup dialog to select a source for the avatar image.

Available options include:

  • URL
  • BLOB Column
  • URL Column

customAttributes.name: Initials
customAttributes.helpText: Select the source column to be displayed within each avatar. Only varchar2 data types are supported.

customAttributes.name: Shape
customAttributes.helpText: Select the shape of the avatar. Default inherits the shape set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Type
customAttributes.helpText: Select the type of content that will be displayed in the avatar.

customAttributes.name: Comment Class
customAttributes.helpText: Allows you to pass a class onto individual comments. Example using is-active will highlight the specific comment with a different shade.

customAttributes.name: Date
customAttributes.helpText: Select a source column containing a date, date range, or time.

customAttributes.name: Comment Text
customAttributes.helpText: Select a source column containing text for user comments.

customAttributes.name: Display Avatar
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to display or hide the avatar for each row. When set to Yes, an avatar will be displayed with the assigned image, icon, or initials. When set to No, no avatar will be displayed.

customAttributes.name: Style
customAttributes.helpText: Set the style of the comments.

customAttributes.name: User Name
customAttributes.helpText: Select a source column to be displayed as the user name associated with a comment.

plugin.name: Content Row

customAttributes.name: Type
customAttributes.helpText: Select the type of content that will be displayed in the avatar.

customAttributes.name: Alignment
customAttributes.helpText: Changes the alignment of the badge within its column.

customAttributes.name: Column Width
customAttributes.helpText: Set the width of the content row columns. Default width is medium.

customAttributes.name: Icon
customAttributes.helpText: Enter an icon class or use the Pick Icon dialog to select the icon you want to add to display in the badge.

customAttributes.name: Label
customAttributes.helpText: Enter a value for the badge label or select a source column from the quick pick options.

customAttributes.name: Display Label
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to display or hide the badge label. When set to Yes, the badge label will be displayed in addition to the badge value. When set to No, only the badge value will be displayed.

customAttributes.name: Position
customAttributes.helpText: Positions the Badge column to either the "Start" or "End" of the row.

customAttributes.name: Shape
customAttributes.helpText: Select the shape of the badge. Default inherits the shape set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Size
customAttributes.helpText: Set the size of the badge. The default inherits the sizing set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: State
customAttributes.helpText: Select the source column for the state of the badge. Substitute column value with "danger", "warning", "success" or "info" for default styling.

customAttributes.name: Style
customAttributes.helpText: Select the badge style. Default inherits the style set by the containing template.

customAttributes.name: Value
customAttributes.helpText: Select a source column for the badge value.

customAttributes.name: Description
customAttributes.helpText: Enter some text and/or column substitution strings to be used as the description in each row.

customAttributes.name: Display Avatar
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to display or hide the avatar for each row. When set to Yes, an avatar will be displayed with the assigned image, icon, or initials. When set to No, no avatar will be displayed.

customAttributes.name: Display Badge
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to display or hide the badge for each row. When set to Yes, a badge will be displayed in each row. When set to No, no badge will be displayed.

customAttributes.name: Icon
customAttributes.helpText: Enter an icon class or use the Pick Icon dialog to select the icon you want to add to display in the avatar.

customAttributes.name: Title
customAttributes.helpText: Enter a concise and descriptive title for the group of items. The title should summarize the contents or purpose of the group, making it easy to understand at a glance.

customAttributes.name: Item CSS Classes
customAttributes.helpText: Enter CSS classes to add to the items. You may add multiple classes by separating them with a space.

customAttributes.name: Miscellaneous
customAttributes.helpText: Enter miscellaneous content such as additional text and/or column substitution strings to display in each row.

customAttributes.name: Overline
customAttributes.helpText: Enter some text and/or column substitution strings to display at the top of each row above the title and description.

customAttributes.name: Stack on Mobile
customAttributes.helpText: Stack the content row elements at the mobile breakpoints.

customAttributes.name: Title
customAttributes.helpText: Enter some text and/or column substitution strings to be used as the title in each row.

customAttributes.name: Align-Content
customAttributes.helpText: Align-content controls how multiple flex lines are distributed along the cross axis. It only has an effect when the container wraps (flex-wrap: wrap) and there is extra space in the cross axis.

customAttributes.name: Align-Items
customAttributes.helpText: Align-items sets the default cross-axis alignment for all flex items within each line. It applies inside a line; when wrapping, align-content controls spacing between lines. Per-item overrides use align-self.

customAttributes.name: Direction
customAttributes.helpText: Flex-direction sets the main axis of a flex container and the visual order of flex items. This affects which axis justify-content (main axis) and align-items/align-content (cross axis) operate on. It does not change DOM order, reading order, or tab order-only visual layout.

customAttributes.name: Flex Item Sizing
customAttributes.helpText: Good for sidebars / controls that must not expand.

customAttributes.name: Gap
customAttributes.helpText: The gap property sets the space between rows and columns of items.

customAttributes.name: Justify-Content
customAttributes.helpText: Justify-content distributes free space along the main axis (set by flex-direction). On wrapped containers, it applies within each line; align-content controls spacing between lines.

customAttributes.name: Overflow
customAttributes.helpText: Controls what happens when content exceeds its container's size, letting you choose whether it scrolls, stays visible, or gets clipped.

customAttributes.name: Wrap
customAttributes.helpText: Flex-wrap controls whether flex items stay on a single line or can flow onto multiple lines along the cross axis. When wrapping occurs, align-content can distribute space between the lines. Without wrapping, align-content has no effect.

customAttributes.name: Apply Theme Colors
customAttributes.helpText: Specify whether to use the Universal Theme colors for avatar and badge row items. When set to Yes, the u-colors class will be applied to each row. When set to No, a default color will be used instead.

customAttributes.name: Layout

customAttributes.name: Title

customAttributes.helpText: Apply typography utility classes or custom CSS to the metric card meta attribute (for example, u-text-heading-sm). You may add multiple classes by separating them with a space.
